data.title: Digital pathology market valued at USD 2.01 billion in 2026, set to reach over USD 3.2 billion by 2031 with a 9.9% CAGR as AI adoption rises - Mordor Intelligence.
data.description: In Hyderabad, India, as of January 16, 2026, the digital pathology market has reached a value of USD 2.01 billion. Based on data from Mordor Intelligence, it is expected to exceed USD 3.2 billion by 2031, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 9.90% predicted for the forecast period spanning from 2026 to 2031. This market trend signifies substantial growth and potential opportunities within the industry.

data.title: transcosmos partners to enhance citizens' health in line with Ministry's Basic Policy for Health Improvement.
data.description: Tokyo, January 16, 2026 - In a joint effort with partner companies, the Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare in Japan has launched comprehensive initiatives to improve national health. The Basic Policy for the Promotion of National Health Improvement aims to enhance citizens' well-being by providing a one-stop solution. The collaboration involves various organizations and businesses, focusing on key areas such as healthcare, lifestyle, and preventive measures. This initiative targets a wide range of participants, with plans to impact millions of residents across the country. Through this partnership, the ministry aims to address current health challenges and promote a healthier future for all individuals in Japan.

data.title: INCHEON-IFEZ Booth at CES 2026 ends with $560M in export consultations, 17 awards, 15K visitors, and 50 innovation companies.
data.description: More than 15,000 individuals visited the INCHEON–IFEZ Booth, where the Incheon Free Economic Zone (IFEZ) aided a total of 50 innovative companies in showcasing their products at CES 2026. These participating companies have established a solid groundwork for global expansion, with 20 Memorandums of Understanding (MoUs) signed during the event. This promising development took place in INCHEON, South Korea on January 16, 2026.

European Movers: Winners: Close Brothers (CBG LN) +8.9%, Alstom (ALO FP) +1.8%, Swissquote (SQN SW) +0.8% Losers: Richemont (CFR SW) -1.7%, Swiss Re (SREN SW) -1.6%, Banca Monte Paschi (BMPS IM) -1.4%

data.title: Construction permits received for 2 grocery stores in Vilnius and Pabradė by AB Tewox companies; secured long-term leases.
data.description: In Vilnius, Lithuania, on January 16, 2026, it was announced that two businesses overseen by AB Tewox have been granted licenses for the establishment of two fresh supermarket outlets in Vilnius and Pabradė. These ventures have also finalized extended lease contracts, ensuring their sustainability.
